conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I have a child, Alex, who's 7 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask you a few more questions.

Are you responsible for Alex? This means you live with Alex and are responsible for their care, such as providing for their food, clothing, and general wellbeing.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Alex doesn't live with me, but I do contribute to their upkeep. I help out with things like clothes, food, and other expenses regularly.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. When a child doesn't live with you, the situation requires some additional information.

Does Alex live with someone else who is also contributing to their upkeep (such as another parent, relative, or guardian)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with someone else who looks after them day-to-day.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. This is an important factor in determining eligibility.

Is the person Alex lives with claiming Child Benefit for Alex, or do you know if they intend to claim it?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, they're not claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and as far as I know, they don't intend to claim it.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I have a few more questions to fully assess your eligibility.

Even though Alex doesn't live with you, do you contribute at least the same amount (or more) towards Alex's upkeep as the person Alex lives with? In other words, are you contributing an equal or greater amount for Alex's care compared to the person they live with?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I contribute at least as much as the person Alex lives with - I give £50 a week towards Alex's upkeep.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. I have just a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

Is Alex under 16 years old, or if they're 16 or over, are they in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is 7 years old, so yes, under 16.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. One final question to complete the assessment:

Are you living in the UK, or do you qualify under specific circumstances that allow you to claim while living abroad (such as working for a UK employer or certain Crown servants)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm based in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:

✓ **Alex meets the age requirement** - At 7 years old, Alex is under 16

✓ **You contribute sufficiently** - You're contributing at least as much to Alex's upkeep (£50/week) as the person Alex lives with

✓ **No one else is claiming** - The person Alex lives with is not claiming Child Benefit and doesn't intend to

✓ **You meet the residency requirement** - You live in the UK (Manchester)

Even though Alex doesn't live with you, you can still claim Child Benefit because you're contributing at least as much towards their care as the person they live with, and no one else is claiming for Alex.

You should be able to proceed with making a claim for Child Benefit for Alex.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 7 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £50.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
circumstances: Claimant contributes £50.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 7 years old, which means they are under 16 and meet the age requirement for Child Benefit. Although Alex does not live with the claimant, the claimant contributes at least as much towards Alex's upkeep (£50 per week) as the person Alex lives with. The person Alex lives with is not claiming Child Benefit and does not intend to claim it. The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester), satisfying the residency requirement. According to the rules, only one person can get Child Benefit for a child, and when a child doesn't live with the claimant, they can still claim if they contribute at least as much to the child's upkeep as anyone else and no one else is claiming. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex. Although Alex doesn't live with them, the claimant contributes at least as much as the person Alex lives with (£50/week), no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, the child is under 16, and the claimant lives in the UK. All eligibility requirements are satisfied.
